Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Department of Public Works and Government Services Act (community benefit)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Department of Public Works and Government Services Act (community benefit)

Stages.StageText: Reprinted as amended by the Standing Committee on Transport, Infrastructure and Communities as a working copy for the use of the House of Commons at Report Stage and as reported to the House on DecemberÂ 5, 2016

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Department of Public Works and Government Services Act to provide the Minister with the authority to require an assessment of the benefits that a community derives from a construction, maintenance or repair project.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The Department of Public Works and Government Services Act is amended by adding the following after section 20:

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of this section, community benefit means a social, economic or environmental benefit that a community derives from a construction, maintenance or repair project, and includes local job creation and training opportunities, improvement of public space within the community and any other specific benefit identified by the community.

The Minister may, before awarding a contract for the construction, maintenance or repair of public works, federal real property or federal immovables, require bidders on the proposal to provide information on the community benefits that the project will provide.
A contracting party shall, upon request by the Minister, provide the Minister with an assessment as to whether the project has provided community benefits.

The Minister shall cause to be tabled before each House of Parliament, within 90 days after the end of each fiscalââyearââor,ââifââParliamentââis not then sitting, on any ofthe first 90 days next thereafter that Parliament is sitting, a report on community benefits provided by construction, maintenance or repair projects.
